# Spec Readiness Checklist

Use this checklist before moving from spec to technical plan.

## Scope and intent

- [ ] Objective is explicit and measurable.
- [ ] Context and problem are documented.
- [ ] Scope and out-of-scope are explicit.
- [ ] Assumptions are explicit (not hidden as facts).
- [ ] Open questions are either resolved or marked as blockers.

## Behavior definition

- [ ] Functional requirements are clear and testable.
- [ ] Business rules are explicit.
- [ ] UI states are defined (or marked `Not applicable`).
- [ ] Edge cases are listed.
- [ ] Acceptance criteria are testable (`Given/When/Then` or equivalent).

## Technical and delivery readiness

- [ ] Non-functional requirements are explicit.
- [ ] Technical constraints are documented.
- [ ] Risks are listed with mitigation.
- [ ] High-level implementation plan is included.
- [ ] PR breakdown is small, reviewable, and sequenced.

## Validation and evidence

- [ ] Validation plan includes real commands/checks.
- [ ] Expected evidence is explicit (outputs, changed files, criteria coverage, risk notes).
- [ ] No requirement/spec conflict is detected.

## Decision

Mark one:

- [ ] Ready for technical plan
- [ ] Needs clarification
- [ ] Too broad (requires scope split)
- [ ] Blocked by missing context
